title: Deep Generative Design for Mass Production
abstract: Despite its success, generative design (GD) faces significant challenges regarding the manufacturability of complex designs, often necessitating extensive manual modifications due to limitations in standard manufacturing processes. This paper proposes a deep generative design framework that integrates manufacturing constraints directly into the generative process.
method_summary: Proposes a deep generative design framework that embeds manufacturing constraints (injection molding, casting, machining) into the design generation process, enabling mass-producible designs without post-hoc manual modification.

title: A Deep 3D Convolutional Neural Network Based Design for Manufacturability Framework
abstract: Traditionally, design for manufacturability (DFM) rules are hand-crafted and used to accelerate the engineering product design cycle by integrating manufacturability analysis during the design stage. This paper proposes a deep 3D CNN-based DFM framework that learns to predict manufacturability directly from 3D voxelized CAD models.
method_summary: Pioneering work using deep 3D CNNs to learn DFM rules directly from voxelized CAD models, replacing hand-crafted manufacturability rules with learned representations.

title: A Machine-Learning Framework for Design for Manufacturability
abstract: Computer-aided Design for Manufacturing (DFM) systems play an important role in reducing the time taken for product development by providing manufacturability feedback to the designer while a component is being designed. This paper presents a machine learning framework for DFM that can learn from manufacturing data.
method_summary: Introduces a general ML framework for DFM that learns manufacturability assessment from historical manufacturing data, enabling automated feedback during the design phase.

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks-Based Tolerance-Aware Manufacturability Assessment Integrating Design-for-Manufacturing Principles
abstract: This study proposes a methodology that evaluates manufacturability directly from parametric design features, enabling explicit incorporation of dimensional tolerances without requiring computer-aided design (CAD) geometry reconstruction. The approach employs Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KANs) to learn functional relationships between design parameters, tolerances, and manufacturability outcomes.
method_summary: Uses Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KANs) to assess manufacturability from parametric design features with explicit tolerance incorporation, bypassing the need for full CAD geometry reconstruction.

title: McGAN: Generating Manufacturable Designs by Embedding Manufacturing Rules into Conditional Generative Adversarial Network
abstract: Generative design methods aim to automatically generate a wide variety of designs that satisfy functional or aesthetic design requirements. However, research to date generally lacks consideration of manufacturability for the generated designs. This paper proposes a novel GD approach using deep neural networks to encode DFM rules into a conditional GAN.
method_summary: Embeds manufacturing rules (DFM constraints) directly into a conditional GAN architecture, enabling generation of designs that are inherently manufacturable without post-processing.

title: Enhancing the Product Quality of the Injection Process Using eXplainable Artificial Intelligence
abstract: This study proposes an optimal injection molding process control system to reduce the defect rate of injection molding products with XAI (eXplainable Artificial Intelligence) approaches. Boosting algorithms are used to predict defect occurrence and SHAP-based explanations identify critical process parameters.
method_summary: Combines boosting ML models with SHAP-based explainability for injection molding defect prediction, enabling interpretable process parameter optimization to reduce defect rates.

title: Explainable Artificial Intelligence for Manufacturing Cost Estimation and Machining Feature Visualization
abstract: The proposed process consists of (1) data collection and pre-processing, (2) 3D deep learning architecture exploration, and (3) visualization to explain the prediction results. The proposed deep learning model predicts manufacturing cost directly from 3D CAD models.
method_summary: Uses 3D deep learning on CAD models for manufacturing cost estimation with explainable AI visualization of machining features that drive cost predictions.

title: Machine Learning-Based Manufacturing Cost Prediction from 2D Engineering Drawings via Geometric Features
abstract: We present an integrated machine learning framework that transforms how manufacturing cost is estimated from 2D engineering drawings. Unlike traditional quotation workflows that require labor-intensive manual feature extraction, our approach automatically extracts geometric features from drawings.
method_summary: Automates manufacturing cost prediction from 2D engineering drawings by extracting geometric features and applying ML models, replacing labor-intensive manual quotation workflows.

title: Real-time prediction and adaptive adjustment of continuous casting based on deep learning
abstract: To address the challenge of real-time quality control in continuous casting, surrogate models are developed to replace high-fidelity simulations with acceptable accuracy and less computational time and cost. This work presents a deep learning-based surrogate for real-time casting defect prediction and adaptive process adjustment.
method_summary: Develops a deep learning surrogate model for real-time continuous casting quality prediction, enabling adaptive process adjustment with orders-of-magnitude speedup over physics simulations.

title: Data-Driven Prediction of Casting Defects in Magnesium High-Pressure Die Casting Using Machine Learning
abstract: The aim was to predict the presence of defect classes (cold flow, shrinkage cavity, blister, soldering point, scrap) in magnesium high-pressure die casting using machine learning models trained on process data.
method_summary: Applies ML classifiers to predict multiple casting defect types in Mg die casting from process parameters, enabling proactive quality control.

title: Springback prediction using machine learning: an application for simplified automotive body-in-white structures
abstract: Nonlinear FEA is used to predict springback in sheet metal forming, but it is time-consuming and requires specialized expertise. Machine learning holds the promise of providing rapid and accurate springback predictions for design exploration in automotive body-in-white structures.
method_summary: Trains ML models as surrogates for nonlinear FEA to rapidly predict springback in automotive sheet metal forming, enabling faster design exploration.

title: An Artificial Intelligence Application for In-Process Springback Control of Sheet Metal Bending
venue: ASME J. Manufacturing Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper presents an AI application for in-process springback control of sheet metal bending, using neural networks to predict and compensate for springback in real-time during the bending process.
method_summary: Applies neural networks for real-time in-process springback prediction and compensation during sheet metal bending, closing the loop between sensing and control.

title: Advancing Additive Manufacturing through Deep Learning: A Comprehensive Review of Current Progress and Future Challenges
abstract: This paper presents the first comprehensive literature review of deep learning (DL) applications in additive manufacturing (AM). It addresses the need for a thorough analysis in this rapidly growing area, covering design optimization, process monitoring, defect detection, and quality control.
method_summary: Comprehensive survey of deep learning in additive manufacturing covering design, process optimization, monitoring, and quality control, identifying trends toward generative models and physics-informed approaches.

title: Concurrent Build Direction, Part Segmentation, and Topology Optimization for Additive Manufacturing Using Neural Networks
abstract: We propose a neural network-based approach to topology optimization that aims to reduce the use of support structures in additive manufacturing. Our approach uses a network architecture that allows the concurrent optimization of build direction, part segmentation, and topology.
method_summary: Uses neural networks to concurrently optimize build direction, part segmentation, and topology for AM, significantly reducing support structure requirements.

title: Graph Neural Network-Based Topology Optimization for Self-Supporting Structures in Additive Manufacturing
abstract: This paper presents a machine learning-based framework for topology optimization of self-supporting structures, specifically tailored for additive manufacturing (AM). By employing a graph neural network (GNN), the framework learns to generate optimized topologies that satisfy overhang angle constraints.
method_summary: Proposes a GNN-based topology optimization framework that directly generates self-supporting structures for AM, eliminating the need for iterative overhang constraint enforcement.

title: Learn to Rotate: Part Orientation for Reducing Support Volume via Generalizable Reinforcement Learning
abstract: In design for additive manufacturing, an essential task is to determine the optimal build orientation of a part according to one or multiple factors. Heuristic search is used by most part orientation algorithms but is computationally expensive. This work proposes a generalizable reinforcement learning approach for part orientation optimization.
method_summary: Applies reinforcement learning to learn a generalizable policy for part build orientation optimization in AM, reducing support volume while being computationally efficient at inference time.

title: A Reinforcement Learning Approach for Process Parameter Optimization in Additive Manufacturing
abstract: This paper presents a reinforcement learning approach for optimizing process parameters in additive manufacturing. The RL agent learns to select optimal parameters (laser power, scan speed, layer thickness) to minimize defects while maintaining part quality.
method_summary: Formulates AM process parameter optimization as an RL problem, where an agent learns to dynamically adjust laser power, scan speed, and other parameters to minimize defects.

title: Multiscale Topology Optimization of Functionally Graded Lattice Structures Based on Physics-Augmented Neural Network Material Models
abstract: We present a new framework for the simultaneous optimization of both the topology as well as the relative density grading of cellular structures and materials, also known as lattices. Due to manufacturing constraints in additive manufacturing, the framework incorporates physics-augmented neural network material models.
method_summary: Combines physics-augmented neural networks with multiscale topology optimization to simultaneously optimize lattice topology and density grading under AM manufacturing constraints.

title: Generative Artificial Intelligence in Lattice Structure Design for Additive Manufacturing: A Critical Review
abstract: AI enables automated inverse design, generating lattice structures that meet predefined functional and mechanical targets, reducing trial-and-error efforts. It supports performance optimization by enhancing mechanical, thermal, and acoustic properties through data-driven approaches.
method_summary: Reviews generative AI methods (GANs, VAEs, diffusion models) for lattice structure design in AM, covering inverse design, performance optimization, and process-aware generation.

title: LatticeGraphNet: A Two-Scale Graph Neural Operator for Simulating Lattice Structures
abstract: This study introduces a two-scale Graph Neural Operator (GNO), namely LatticeGraphNet (LGN), designed as a surrogate model for costly nonlinear finite-element simulations of three-dimensional lattice structures.
method_summary: Proposes a two-scale graph neural operator as a fast surrogate for nonlinear FE simulation of 3D lattice structures, enabling rapid mechanical response prediction for design optimization.

title: Predictive Modeling of Lattice Structure Design for 316L Stainless Steel Using Machine Learning in the L-PBF Process
abstract: Lattice structures in additive manufacturing of 316L stainless steel have gained increasing attention due to their well-suited mechanical properties and lightweight characteristics. Infill structures play a critical role in determining the mechanical performance of printed parts.
method_summary: Develops ML models to predict mechanical properties of lattice structures in L-PBF from design parameters, enabling rapid design space exploration for 316L stainless steel parts.

title: Deep Learning for Melt Pool Depth Contour Prediction From Surface Thermal Images via Vision Transformers
abstract: Insufficient overlap between the melt pools produced during Laser Powder Bed Fusion (L-PBF) can lead to lack-of-fusion defects and deteriorated mechanical and fatigue performance. In-situ monitoring of melt pool depth is critical but challenging. This work uses Vision Transformers to predict melt pool depth contours from surface thermal images.
method_summary: Applies Vision Transformers to predict subsurface melt pool depth contours from surface thermal images in L-PBF, enabling real-time in-situ monitoring for defect prevention.

title: Additive Manufacturing Processes Protocol Prediction by Artificial Intelligence using X-ray Computed Tomography Data
abstract: The quality of the part fabricated from the Additive Manufacturing (AM) process depends upon the process parameters used, and therefore, optimization is required for apt quality. A methodology is proposed to predict AM process protocols from X-ray CT scan data using AI.
method_summary: Proposes an AI methodology to predict optimal AM process protocols from X-ray CT data, linking internal part quality metrics to process parameter selection.

title: Accelerated Discovery of 3D Printing Materials Using Data-Driven Multiobjective Optimization
abstract: We propose a machine learning approach to accelerating the discovery of additive manufacturing materials with optimal trade-offs in mechanical performance. A multiobjective optimization algorithm automatically navigates the vast design space of material compositions and process parameters.
method_summary: Combines Bayesian multiobjective optimization with ML surrogates to accelerate discovery of 3D printing materials, achieving Pareto-optimal mechanical properties with minimal experiments.

title: Self-supporting Topology Optimization for Additive Manufacturing
abstract: The paper presents a topology optimization approach that designs an optimal structure, called a self-supporting structure, which is ready to be fabricated via additive manufacturing without the usage of additional support structures.
method_summary: Introduces an overhang angle constraint formulation for topology optimization that directly produces self-supporting structures for AM, eliminating support structure requirements.

title: Holistic Review on Design for Additive Manufacturing
abstract: The review explores DfAM potential, tools, standards, and applications and key insights/inferences are presented. The review presented 14 major DfAM insights and suggested areas for further development across design methods, tools, and standards.
method_summary: Comprehensive review of DfAM covering design methods, computational tools, standards, and industrial applications, identifying 14 key insights and future research directions.

title: Hybrid Deep Learning Cost Evaluation Using CNN with ANN for the Plastic Injection Industry
abstract: These results can predict cost evaluation at about 98.65% accuracy for parts costs, 95.17% accuracy for mould costs, and 96.83% of multi-price for multi-volume. The hybrid CNN-ANN model processes both geometric and process features.
method_summary: Combines CNN (for geometric feature extraction) with ANN (for cost regression) in a hybrid architecture for plastic injection molding cost estimation, achieving over 95% accuracy.

title: Sample-Efficient Bayesian Transfer Learning for Online Machine Parameter Optimization
abstract: This work introduces a method to optimize the machine parameters in the system itself using a Bayesian optimization (BO) algorithm. By leveraging existing machine data, we use a transfer learning approach to achieve sample-efficient online parameter optimization.
method_summary: Combines Bayesian optimization with transfer learning for sample-efficient online optimization of manufacturing machine parameters, leveraging prior machine data to reduce required experiments.

title: An Additive Manufacturing Optimal Control Framework Demonstrated in Minimizing Layer-Level Thermal Variance in Electron Beam Powder Bed Fusion
abstract: Additive manufacturing (AM) techniques hold promise but face significant challenges in process planning and optimization. The large temporal and spatial variations in temperature that can occur in layer-by-layer fabrication motivate optimal control approaches.
method_summary: Proposes an optimal control framework for AM that minimizes layer-level thermal variance in electron beam PBF using learned thermal models, improving part quality consistency.
